target.name: Physical Intelligence
target.founder: Karol Hausman, Sergey Levine, Chelsea Finn, Brian Ichter

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $5.6B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $500M - $32.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Safe Superintelligence
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $32B

private.name: World Labs
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $5B+

private.name: Nominal

private.name: Photoroom

data.narrative:
Physical Intelligence is a Series B AI & Software company founded by Karol Hausman, Sergey Levine, Chelsea Finn, and Brian Ichter, currently valued at $5.6B post-money following $600M+ in total capital raised. The relevant peer universe comprises 9 private AI companies at the same stage plus 1 public comparable (Palantir), providing a mixed but relevant framework for triangulation.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the typical Series B step-up multiplier of 1.9x applied to the $5.6B last post-money valuation, implying $10.6B. Peer-median analysis references 4 same-sector, same-stage companies: Safe Superintelligence at $32.0B, World Labs at $5.0B, Nominal at $1.0B, and Photoroom at $500M, yielding a median of $3.0B and a range spanning $500M to $32.0B. The wide peer dispersion reflects acute heterogeneity in AI & Software Series B valuations.

Triangulation across these two methods produces a range of $2.5B (low) to $12.2B (high), with a median of $6.8B. The current $5.6B valuation sits below the triangulated median, though within the dispersed peer set. Key uncertainties include the relevance of extreme peer valuations—Safe Superintelligence at $32.0B materially affects stage-progression assumptions—and the applicability of public market comps to early-stage AI software. Revenue traction, technical differentiation, and path to commercialization remain unmeasured drivers of the wide valuation range observed.
